edges.evidence_quote: The origins of Los Zetas date back to the late 1990s, when commandos of the Mexican Army deserted their ranks and began working as the enforcement arm of the Gulf Cartel.
edges.context: Los Zetas originally served as the enforcement arm of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: In February 2010, Los Zetas broke away and formed their own criminal organization, rivalling the Gulf Cartel.
edges.context: Los Zetas split from the Gulf Cartel and became rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: They were at one point Mexico's largest and most expansive drug cartel in terms of geographical presence, overtaking their rivals, the Sinaloa Cartel, in physical territory.
edges.context: Los Zetas and Sinaloa Cartel are rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: As of March 2016, Grupo Bravo (Bravo Group), Los Talibanes (The Talibans), and Zetas Vieja Escuela (Old School Zetas) had formed an alliance with the Gulf Cartel against Cártel del Noreste (Cartel of the Northeast).
edges.context: Grupo Bravo allied with Gulf Cartel against Cártel del Noreste.

edges.evidence_quote: As of March 2016, Grupo Bravo (Bravo Group), Los Talibanes (The Talibans), and Zetas Vieja Escuela (Old School Zetas) had formed an alliance with the Gulf Cartel against Cártel del Noreste (Cartel of the Northeast).
edges.context: Los Talibanes allied with Gulf Cartel against Cártel del Noreste.

edges.evidence_quote: Consequently, Los Zetas allied with the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel, the Juárez Cartel, and the Tijuana Cartel.
edges.context: Los Zetas allied with Beltrán-Leyva Cartel after the split from Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: When the hostilities began, the Cartel joined forces with its former rivals, the Sinaloa Cartel and La Familia Michoacana, aiming to take out Los Zetas.
edges.context: Gulf Cartel allied with Sinaloa Cartel against Los Zetas.

edges.evidence_quote: When the hostilities began, the Cartel joined forces with its former rivals, the Sinaloa Cartel and La Familia Michoacana, aiming to take out Los Zetas.

edges.evidence_quote: In response to the rising power of the Gulf Cartel, the rival Sinaloa Cartel established Los Negros, an enforcer group similar to Los Zetas but not as complex or successful.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel created Los Negros as its enforcer group.

edges.evidence_quote: Another splinter group was formed also named Sangre Nueva Zeta (New Blood Zeta), allying themselves with the Jalisco Cartel as an armed wing.
edges.context: Sangre Nueva Zeta allied with Jalisco Cartel as an armed wing.

edges.evidence_quote: The origins of Los Zetas date back to the late 1990s, when commandos of the Mexican Army deserted their ranks and began working as the enforcement arm of the Gulf Cartel.
edges.context: Los Zetas originally worked as the enforcement arm of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: In February 2010, Los Zetas broke away and formed their own criminal organization, rivalling the Gulf Cartel.
edges.context: Los Zetas broke away and became rivals of the Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: They were at one point Mexico's largest and most expansive drug cartel in terms of geographical presence, overtaking their rivals, the Sinaloa Cartel, in physical territory.
edges.context: Los Zetas were rivals of the Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Following the conflict with the Gulf Cartel, Los Zetas joined forces with the Beltrán Leyva Cartel (who was simultaneously separating from the Sinaloa Cartel) as well as the Juarez Cartel and Tijuana Cartel or Arellano Félix organization, to counteract the alliance of the Gulf Cartel, Sinaloa Cartel and La Familia Michoacana cartels.
edges.context: Los Zetas allied with the Beltrán Leyva Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Following the conflict with the Gulf Cartel, Los Zetas joined forces with the Beltrán Leyva Cartel (who was simultaneously separating from the Sinaloa Cartel) as well as the Juarez Cartel and Tijuana Cartel or Arellano Félix organization, to counteract the alliance of the Gulf Cartel, Sinaloa Cartel and La Familia Michoacana cartels.

edges.evidence_quote: Early in 2012 it was reported that Los Zetas were operating in the northern Venezuela – Colombia border, and had teamed up with the Colombian outfit called Los Rastrojos.
edges.context: Los Zetas teamed up with Los Rastrojos to control drug trafficking routes.

edges.evidence_quote: They were active in Europe, specifically in Italy with the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.context: Los Zetas were active in Europe with the 'Ndrangheta.
